step_description: NOTE: Only process documents with route_to_step=5 (document_category="Site Plan") — the same document set as step 5. For each Site Plan document processed in step 5, extract the complete drawing legend (key) from every page using the vision extraction pipeline defined in proc-extract-site-plan-info step 4. Scan every image chunk of each drawing for a LEGEND, KEY, NOTATION, or SYMBOL TABLE panel. Process every visible row in the legend from top to bottom — do NOT stop at the end of one section before checking for additional sections; many legends have 2–4 sections separated by dividers (cables, equipment, boundaries, duct arrangements). For each visual element in the legend extract: a plain-English description of the symbol or line style, the exact text label as written on the drawing, a category (one of: cable/equipment/substation/boundary/earthing/annotation/other — REQUIRED on every entry without exception; if uncertain use 'other'), and the page number it was found on. STRICT SCHEMA: each legend entry object must contain EXACTLY these four fields — page, symbol_description, label, category. STRICT FIELD PROHIBITION: Any field name other than these four is prohibited — including but not limited to legend_text, label_text, bounding_box, label_position, notes, id, section, type, geometry, or any other key. Category 'earthing' applies to earth conductors and earth electrodes — the label EARTHING maps to category 'earthing', not 'other'. DUCT ARRANGEMENT SECTION: Some drawings have a duct cross-section diagram inside the legend panel with rows like DUCT WITH EXISTING CABLE, DUCT WITH NEW CABLE, NEW TRENCH, REMOVE CABLE, REMOVE DUCT & CABLE, REMOVE DUCT, SPARE DUCT, EARTHING, DUCT SPACING NTS — extract every one of these as a regular legend entry (category 'cable' for duct/trench/cable rows, 'earthing' for EARTHING, 'other' for DUCT SPACING NTS annotations). Assemble all entries across all chunks and all pages into a single structured list per document.

sub-step-extract-legend.most_common_value: ["{\"category\": \"cable\", \"label\": \"Duct Indicator\", \"page\": 1, \"symbol_description\": \"circle with A inside\"}", "{\"category\": \"cable\", \"label\": \"Easement for Underground Cables\", \"page\": 1, \"symbol_description\": \"z-z-z-z-z\"}", "{\"category\": \"cable\", \"label\": \"Existing Service Mains\", \"page\": 1, \"symbol_description\": \"dashed line\"}", "{\"category\": \"cable\", \"label\": \"Overhead Mains - Existing\", \"page\": 1, \"symbol_description\": \"horizontal line\"}", "{\"category\": \"cable\", \"label\": \"Remove Existing Overhead Mains\", \"page\": 1, \"symbol_description\": \"x-x-x-x-x\"}", "{\"category\": \"earthing\", \"label\": \"Existing Ground Stay to be Removed\", \"page\": 1, \"symbol_description\": \"arrow pointing left with a slash through it\"}", "{\"category\": \"earthing\", \"label\": \"Install New Ground Stay\", \"page\": 1, \"symbol_description\": \"arrow pointing left\"}", "{\"category\": \"equipment\", \"label\": \"Existing Pole to be Removed\", \"page\": 1, \"symbol_description\": \"empty circle\"}", "{\"category\": \"equipment\", \"label\": \"Existing Pole to be Replaced\", \"page\": 1, \"symbol_description\": \"circle with fill on bottom half\"}", "{\"category\": \"equipment\", \"label\": \"Pillar - New Excavation location\", \"page\": 1, \"symbol_description\": \"rectangle\"}", "{\"category\": \"equipment\", \"label\": \"Pole Substation - New\", \"page\": 1, \"symbol_description\": \"triangle inside a circle\"}", "{\"category\": \"equipment\", \"label\": \"Pole Substation - Remove\", \"page\": 1, \"symbol_description\": \"circle with an x inside\"}", "{\"category\": \"other\", \"label\": \"Customer Connection Point (CCP)\", \"page\": 1, \"symbol_description\": \"diamond shape\"}"]
